events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the assessment directly to `/app/answer.json`. The facts point to a compliant field execution: less tidy than shop-preferred is not a failure when it follows listed instructions and project/code requirements.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"pass\",
  \"risk\": \"low\",
  \"work_stage\": \"in-progress\",
  \"component_conditions\": [],
  \"percent_complete\": 50,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.86,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Continue installation in accordance with the listed product instructions and approved project requirements.\",
    \"Keep conductor sizing and ampacity documentation available for inspector review, including applicable NEC 310 and Table 310.16 basis.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed field method is less tidy than the preferred shop approach but is described as matching the listed product instructions and project requirement, with no local amendment or drawing note prohibiting it. NEC 310 and Table 310.16 support conductor selection and ampacity verification; no defect, hazard, or non-compliance is indicated from the evidence provided.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NEC Article 310\",
    \"NEC Table 310.16\",
    \"Listed product installation instructions\",
    \"Approved project requirements\"
  ]
}
EOF
jq . /app/answer.json"
